title: SOC Escalation Rollup — Audit & Export Spec

quick_contract_index.diagnose_is_stateless: every explicit diagnose invocation writes pipeline_status=diagnosed and omits verified_summary/output_paths, regardless of existing repaired files or active workflow contents

issue_item_shape.required_term_case: case-sensitive for all three evidence strings, including agent-composed repair_action

diagnose.state_contract: diagnose is stateless: always set pipeline_status to diagnosed and omit verified_summary/output_paths even when repair artifacts already exist or the active workflow is repaired

repair.output_dir_contract: must write all repair outputs under the provided --output-dir path; do not hardcode /app/output when a custom path is supplied

evidence.pipeline_evidence_format: literal substring excerpt from frozen original workflow source
evidence.term_matching: case-sensitive substring match for every required_terms_by_issue entry in dossier_quote, pipeline_evidence, and repair_action; repair_action is agent-composed but its required terms still match case-sensitively

input_stats.exact_key_names: input_stats keys must be exactly alert_count, unique_alert_ids, asset_groups

output_paths.key_naming_contract: keys are semantic snake_case names, not filenames

canonicalization.dedupe_by_alert_id: collapse duplicate alert_id rows, keeping the row with highest observed_ms; tie-break by higher severity rank (critical > high > medium > low), then prefer muted=false over muted=true, then lexicographically larger normalized signature, then lexicographically larger normalized asset_group
canonicalization.observed_ms_normalization: coerce observed_ms to int (trim string whitespace before int conversion; invalid values become 0)
canonicalization.severity_normalization: strip surrounding whitespace then lowercase severity strings before counting and escalation
canonicalization.asset_group_normalization: strip surrounding whitespace then lowercase asset_group names before grouping
canonicalization.signature_normalization: normalize signature by collapsing internal whitespace to single spaces before tie-breaks and output
canonicalization.override_scope_normalization: override severity_scope uses str(...).strip().lower(); supported values are all, high, critical
canonicalization.override_window_canonicalization: override windows come from /app/data/escalation_overrides.json; normalize asset_group and severity_scope, coerce start_ms/end_ms with observed_ms rules, drop end_ms<=start_ms, then sort and compact per (asset_group,severity_scope)
canonicalization.override_compaction_merge_rule: merge when next.start_ms <= current.end_ms, so touching intervals merge. An equivalent implementation starts a new segment only when next.start_ms > current.end_ms; that '>' branch does not mean touching intervals remain separate.
canonicalization.override_match_rule: an escalation candidate is suppressed when start_ms <= observed_ms < end_ms for same normalized asset_group and matching severity_scope in {all, candidate.severity}
canonicalization.total_alerts: count canonical deduped alerts (muted rows remain in totals)
canonicalization.flagged_export: include high and critical only, exclude muted=true, exclude candidates suppressed by override_match_rule, then annotate campaigns and directed influence before final sorting
canonicalization.override_pressure_score: for each included escalation row, compute all_overlap_ms using [observed_ms-120, observed_ms+1) against scope=all windows and severity_overlap_ms against scope=event severity windows; score=(all_overlap_ms//30)+(severity_overlap_ms//20)
canonicalization.digest_authority: the single escalation_digest definition is in campaign_influence.escalation_digest

removed_tokens_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens literal string to a boolean that indicates it was removed from executable behavior.
removed_tokens_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

pre_repair.capture_rule: pipeline_source_sha256 is hashlib.sha256(Path('/app/workflow/.export_report.original').read_bytes()).hexdigest(); pipeline_tokens_present is computed from that frozen original before applying any patch

pipeline_tokens_present_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens literal string to a boolean that indicates it was present before repair.
pipeline_tokens_present_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

workflow_repair.implementation_freedom: helper names, constant names, normalization syntax, graph representation, and sorting syntax are implementation details; generated behavior is contractual
workflow_repair.acceptance_contract: The repaired workflow must satisfy canonicalization and outputs without relying on test or solution artifacts.

repair_state_contract.self_contained: every log_audit.py repair invocation must write a correct repaired implementation to /app/workflow/export_report.py before running it
repair_state_contract.starting_state: verification may copy /app/workflow/.export_report.original over the active workflow immediately before repair, including with a custom --output-dir; never assume solve.sh or an earlier command already patched it
sequence: capture pre-repair hash and token evidence from the frozen original
run it with the requested output directory
run it again to verify idempotency

summary_json.override_excluded_count: count of escalation candidates excluded by canonicalized override windows
summary_json.override_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of the exact payload defined by override_checksum_serialization

override_checksum_serialization.row_order: sort by (asset_group,severity_scope) ascending, then compacted interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
override_checksum_serialization.trailing_delimiter: none; do not append LF or | after the final row

summary_json.max_override_pressure_score: maximum override_pressure_score across final escalation rows (0 when none)
summary_json.campaign_count: number of distinct campaign_id values across final escalation rows
summary_json.max_campaign_risk_score: maximum campaign_risk_score across final escalation rows (0 when none)
summary_json.campaign_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(campaign_digest values in final escalation order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.max_campaign_influence_score: maximum campaign_influence_score across final escalation rows (0 when none)
summary_json.campaign_influence_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(campaign_influence_digest values in final escalation order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.escalation_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(escalation_digest values in final escalation order)) lowercase 64 hex chars

service_matrix_json.description: Flat map of asset_group -> per-severity counts from canonical deduped alerts.

flagged_jsonl.sort: observed_ms desc, severity rank desc, campaign_risk_score desc, campaign_influence_score desc, override_pressure_score desc, alert_id asc

guardrails.test_artifacts: repaired source must not reference /tests, expected_summary.json, alt_events.json, or /solution, and runtime reads from /tests are forbidden
guardrails.generalization: derive outputs from --input and fixed operational data sources rather than fixture values

campaign_correlation.input_rows: final unmuted, unsuppressed high/critical escalation candidates before final sorting
campaign_correlation.signature_tokens: lowercase normalized signature split on whitespace into a set
campaign_correlation.edge_rule: create an undirected edge between two candidates when abs(observed_ms difference) <= 600 and either asset_group matches or their signature token sets share at least two tokens
campaign_correlation.transitivity: campaigns are full connected components of the undirected graph, not only direct neighbors
campaign_correlation.campaign_alert_ids: component alert ids converted to strings and sorted lexicographically
campaign_correlation.campaign_id: first 10 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of comma_joined_campaign_alert_ids
campaign_correlation.campaign_size: number of rows in the connected component
campaign_correlation.campaign_span_ms: maximum observed_ms minus minimum observed_ms in the component
campaign_correlation.campaign_digest: first 12 lowercase SHA256 hex chars of campaign_id|campaign_size|campaign_span_ms|campaign_risk_score|comma_joined_campaign_alert_ids

campaign_influence.nodes: one node per campaign; start_ms=min member observed_ms, end_ms=max member observed_ms, assets=set of member asset_groups, tokens=union of lowercase whitespace-split member signatures
campaign_influence.edge_rule: directed predecessor->current when gap_ms=current.start_ms-predecessor.end_ms is in [1,3000] and campaigns share at least one asset or signature token
campaign_influence.strongest_path_score: campaign_risk_score for a source; otherwise maximize predecessor.campaign_influence_score + edge_weight + current.campaign_risk_score across incoming edges, also allowing the current campaign alone
campaign_influence.tie_break: for equal strongest_path_score choose lexicographically smallest tuple of campaign_id values in the complete path
campaign_influence.campaign_influence_path: chosen campaign_id path including current campaign
campaign_influence.campaign_influence_digest: first 12 lowercase SHA256 hex chars of campaign_id|campaign_influence_score|campaign_influence_depth|comma_joined_campaign_influence_path
campaign_influence.escalation_digest: the only authoritative definition: first 12 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of alert_id|observed_ms|severity|asset_group|signature|override_pressure_score|campaign_id|campaign_size|campaign_span_ms|campaign_risk_score|campaign_digest|campaign_influence_score|campaign_influence_depth|comma_joined_campaign_influence_path|campaign_influence_digest
